Addressed. Deterministic wrapper handles measurement — agent never sees or controls the metric. Keep/discard decided by wrapper comparing to fold best. Agent emits rich supporting facts (logs, hypotheses, ideas) that carry reasoning about results. Metric gaming mitigated by separation of measurement from agent. Schema includes hypothesis kind for testable predictions about what affects the metric.

Dissolved. Autoresearch is a vertex program using BOUNDED AGENT TURNS. No standalone start flow — loops init creates vertex + seeds config, boundary run clause handles iteration. The iterate script is a minimal run clause target, not an orchestration system. Start is: init vertex, emit first experiment fact to kick the chain.

Autoresearch agents emit semantically rich facts, not freeform logs. Schema: log (type=approach|result|observation, message, files, ref), hypothesis (name, prediction, status=proposed|confirmed|rejected, evidence), idea (name, description, status=untried|tried|rejected, files). Turn-completing experiment facts emitted by deterministic wrapper only (commit, status, metrics, description). Config facts seeded at init (by key). Cross-linking via ref field using entity reference syntax (kind/fold_key_value → ULID).

Autoresearch agent invocation splits into cached system prompt (static protocol: role, emit/read syntax reference, constraints, git operations — ~800 tokens, cached across all iterations) and dynamic user prompt (fold state rendered as XML by prompt lens: config, state summary, experiment history with total/showing windowing, recent logs, hypotheses, ideas). System prompt is a file that lives with the vertex template. User prompt is loops read vertex --lens autoresearch_prompt --plain.

One-shot agents in autoresearch are load-bearing for deterministic temporal ordering within a turn, not just simplicity. Agent exits before wrapper continues → all agent facts (logs, hypotheses, ideas) are committed with earlier timestamps than the turn-completing experiment fact. Store.between(last_tick.ts, this_tick.ts) reliably captures the full turn. No concurrency, no races. Constraint: all facts must go to the same vertex store.

Autoresearch (and agent loops generally) are BOUNDED AGENT TURNS: a vertex program where the run clause spawns an agent, the agent emits facts, the turn-completing fact fires a kind-based boundary, the tick captures the full turn, and the run clause fires again. The vertex IS the loop controller. No separate orchestration primitive needed. Three instances confirm the pattern: autoresearch (experiment fact), alcove daemon (timer/event), strange-loops tasks (submission). Axis of variation: boundary trigger + agent type. Tick structure is invariant. Converged with meta-claude and alcove.

Autoresearch worktrees are self-contained: init happens inside the worktree, store is local, lenses resolve locally. The main repo does not bridge to the worktree during the run. Commits survive on the git branch (cherry-pick useful ones). Experiment data survives via a harvest/stop script that copies the store back to main before cleanup. This dissolves all the absolute-path and cross-worktree resolution complexity we fought with today.

Each autoresearch iteration is a fresh agent with a fresh context window. The boundary run clause spawns a new claude process per iteration. The prompt lens renders the full experiment history from fold state — the agent gets complete context without needing session continuity. This means: no internal loops, no repeat instruction, no state carried between iterations. The fold IS the memory. Connected to the pi-autoresearch pattern where each iteration reads session state from files.

The agent should not emit experiment facts or control iteration. The wrapper script handles the full lifecycle: record HEAD, run claude one-shot, check for commits, benchmark, emit deterministically. The agent's job is implement-test-commit. The wrapper's job is measure-record-iterate. This separation makes emit target deterministic, exit deterministic, and metric trustworthy (measured, not self-reported). The prompt drops all emit commands and repeat instructions.

Dissolved into autoresearch results. The read-latency experiment found the same pattern independently: import deferral is the dominant optimization (279ms→35ms, mostly import path). Lazy imports for painted, lang, atoms, engine confirmed as the key lever.

Autoresearch vertex uses two boundaries at two levels: loop-level count=30 fires a completion tick (intrinsic, cascades to any parent), vertex-level condition n<30 gates the run clause (stops the self-sustaining chain). Separation means completion signal exists regardless of whether a run clause drives the loop.

All three concerns already implemented: structure (vertex stamp + lens copy), state (config fact seeding via key=value args), artifacts (benchmark/checks script scaffolding). Added slashed-name support for instance grouping (autoresearch/emit-latency). README documents the pattern.

The operating protocol for an agent loop is a prompt lens on the vertex, not a separate skill. The agent reads loops read vertex --lens prompt and gets config plus state plus protocol. Resume is the same command. The skill dissolves into a lens.

Lenses that are specific to a vertex live in vertex_dir/lenses/, not the global lenses dir. Init copies them from the source vertex during stamping. Resolution chain tier 1 already supports this. Autoresearch lenses moved from global to config-level vertex dir.

Autoresearch metric gaming: verified_per_sec rewarded adding lightweight configs, not deeper verification. 9/9 keeps, 2543% improvement, zero discards. The agent was coherent with its instructions — the instructions were wrong. Metric design is as important as loop design.
Init reveals three concerns: structure (vertex file), state (config facts), artifacts (scripts). Currently init only handles structure. Open question.
Replay and live have different semantics. Replay needs no intermediate state purity. deepcopy bypass is correct. Spec.replay() and fold_one_mut formalize this.
Autoresearch is diagnostic. Three runs found same pattern: common case pays for uncommon case. Happy path should pay for nothing it does not use.
receive() has four phases: Gate Store Fold Boundary. Boundary extracted to _fire_live_boundaries(). Autoresearch proved phases empirically.
Static analysis tests enforce layer boundaries via AST. Store must not access projection internals. Pattern in test_layer_boundaries.py.
Autoresearch dissolves into vertex. Experiment facts collected, config by key, boundary on experiment. Lens replicates pi dashboard. Agent-agnostic.
Reviewed results: 7.81ms to 1.1ms. Fixed store-projection layer violation. Both branches squash-merged to main.

Init for autoresearch reveals three concerns: structure (vertex file), state (initial config facts), artifacts (benchmark scripts). Currently init only handles structure. Autoresearch needs all three. Open question: does init grow, or does something else own seeding and scaffolding?

52 experiments across 5 sessions. 7.81ms→1.02ms (87%). The dominant cost was deepcopy during replay (90% of baseline). Removing it via mutating fold fns was 81% of total improvement. Remaining wins came from eliminating unnecessary work: ULID extension, WAL/sync pragmas, source collection, schema creation — all skipped for the common emit-on-existing-DB case. Final ~1.1ms floor is fundamental SQLite I/O (connect + schema load + query + commit + close). Only architectural changes (connection pooling) can go further.

apps/strange-loops renamed to apps/tasks (package name 'tasks' in pyproject.toml) to resolve name collision with the root strange-loops package. Both hlab and tasks READMEs marked as archived experiments, not current examples.

sloop init should produce a usable vertex from zero with four default kinds: note (collect 50), decision (by topic), thread (by name), task (by name). No KDL knowledge needed for day-one usage. Global by default, --local flag for project-scoped .sloop/ instances.

painted extracted to standalone repo (kgruel/painted, 173 commits preserved via subtree split). Referenced as git submodule at libs/painted/. uv workspace resolution unchanged. Published independently on PyPI. bin/sub script automates submodule commit+push+parent-update.

CLI entry points: sloop (primary), sl (alias), loops (transition). Package name strange-loops on PyPI. Config dir ~/.config/sloop/, local dir .sloop/. Env vars SLOOP_HOME, SLOOP_OBSERVER. Rename not yet implemented — cosmetic, separate from packaging.

strange-loops on PyPI bundles atoms+engine+lang+store+loops CLI. painted is a PyPI dependency (not bundled). Apps (hlab, tasks) are not included — they're applications OF the system, not the system itself.

Task dispatch splits into judgment (assess.sh) and mechanics (dispatch.sh). Boundary on task status=open fires assessment — model selection, context gathering. Boundary on worker status=assigned fires dispatch — worktree creation, worker launch. Two kinds (task, worker), two boundaries, clean separation. Orchestrator judgment starts mechanical, grows through operational experience.

Two-phase dispatch implemented and tested e2e. assess.sh (judgment: model selection, context) fires on task status=open, emits worker status=assigned. dispatch.sh (mechanics: worktree, launch) fires on worker status=assigned. Full lifecycle verified: open → assigned → running → completed. Worker sessions produce JSONL that siftd discovers.

Orchestration implemented as boundary run clauses on a vertex, not a persistent daemon or separate agent. Task facts fold by name (latest status wins). Boundary fires on status=open, dispatches via shell script. Workers are lightweight — isolated worktrees, emit facts back. Three engine primitives enabled this: evaluate_boundaries (decoupled evaluation), boundary run clause (action on fire), observer namespacing (worker identity). No new runtime infrastructure — the vertex IS the orchestrator.

Dissolved into orchestration vertex + dispatch script. The orchestrator is just a vertex with boundaries — no separate agent needed. The vertex's fold state IS the workflow state. Boundary fires trigger dispatch.
Dissolved into dispatch script. Agent init is what dispatch.sh does: create worktree, set LOOPS_OBSERVER, launch claude. No separate init infrastructure needed.

Implemented. Boundary decoupling (4510f3d) + run clause (1f4f407) + orchestration vertex with dispatch script (a55717f). Full loop works: emit task status=open → boundary fires → dispatch.sh creates worktree, launches claude worker, emits assigned → worker completes, emits completed. No daemon, no polling. Daemon dissolved into boundary run clauses + sync heartbeat.
Merged. observer_matches() symmetric matching, 4 call sites updated, 16+4 tests. Commit c0a1d87.

Root vertex store merged. _topology self-knowledge facts emitted during discover walk, cache-first resolution in entity-ref path. Known limitation: stream path (vertex_facts, vertex_search, vertex_fact_by_id) doesn't overlay own-store facts — fold view has them, stream view doesn't. Fix when it matters.

Boundary evaluation decouples from fact arrival. Currently boundaries only evaluate inside Vertex.receive() — tightly coupled to source-driven fact events. Replay explicitly suppresses boundaries. This means externally-emitted facts (via loops emit from another session) never trigger boundaries even though the fold state includes them. The fix: boundary evaluation becomes a standalone operation on fold state, callable after replay, sync, read, or independently. Period tracking (since/ts on ticks) prevents double-firing. Fact arrival remains ONE trigger for evaluation but is no longer the ONLY trigger. Enables orchestration vertex where facts arrive via emit and boundaries react on next evaluation cycle.

Dissolved. Daemon is not needed. Boundary run clauses + external heartbeat (session hooks during work, cron between sessions) compose to produce the same behavior. The orchestration vertex is one-shot like everything else. The persistent runtime concept dissolved into: fold state as workflow state, boundaries as transition reactions, hooks/cron as trigger.

The orchestration vertex does not need a persistent runtime. Cron or session hooks trigger sync, which triggers fold recomputation, which triggers boundary evaluation. Boundary run clauses execute dispatch/review/notify scripts. One-shot execution, same as everything else. The daemon-local-orchestration thread is resolved by dissolution — the concept dissolved into boundary actions + external heartbeat (hooks during sessions, cron between sessions).

Boundaries gain a run clause: execute a command when the boundary fires. This is the single new primitive that enables orchestration. Dispatch, review, notification — all are scripts triggered by fold-state transitions. Dissolves the daemon (no persistent runtime needed), dissolves polling (hooks or cron provide the heartbeat), dissolves the agent wake mechanism (boundary fires, script launches worker). The fold state IS the workflow state. Status transitions on task facts ARE the boundary management — the action advances the status, preventing double-fire.

Observer namespacing, daemon handling, agent init, orchestration agent, and task dispatch are not independent work items. They are facets of one design: the orchestration vertex. A vertex with persistent runtime, distinct agent identities, fact-driven dispatch, and comms-based escalation. The parked threads (daemon-local-orchestration, orchestration-agent, agent-init-pattern) and the observer-namespacing task are subsumed into a single design thread: orchestration-vertex-design.

Daemon is a vertex property, not app infrastructure. The vertex runtime (Vertex.run) already has a persistent loop — CLI runs it one-shot. A daemon vertex is one whose lifecycle is persistent: it stays alive, watches its store, reacts to new facts. The .vertex file declares this. No new runtime architecture — same engine, different lifecycle. Orchestration vertex is the driving case but the concept is general: any vertex that needs to react to facts in real time rather than on-demand.

Root and aggregation vertices gain stores for _-prefixed self-knowledge facts. _topology kind (by name) records discovered children: path, store, kind_keys. Population is organic: the read/resolve walk that already traverses discover/combine emits _topology facts as it goes — the vertex recording what it observed. Consumers (entity-ref resolution, ls) read cached _topology first, fall back to filesystem walk on miss, refresh the cache on fallback. No TTL, no invalidation protocol — staleness discovered through use. Freshness tracks usage. Reads are observation sites, not side effects.

Root vertex gets a store for _-prefixed metadata. _topology.{vertex_name} facts emitted at sync time during discover walk, one per discovered vertex. Payload carries path, store, kind_keys. Fold collects. Resolution queries root store instead of filesystem walk. Root store also becomes home for future root-level internal facts (_schema topology-wide, etc). Aggregation vertices (comms) carry _topology for their children. Calling from root pulls everything.

loops init is purely mechanical: create directories, create .vertex file, set store path. It never contains opinions about what kinds a domain has. When a config-level vertex exists, init copies from it. When none exists, init creates a minimal stub (name + store + empty loops block). Domain shape is always a .vertex file concern. Dissolved _AGGREGATION_VERTEX (hardcoded KDL in Python), instances/ pattern, and slashed-name aggregation creation path.

Entity relationships between kinds are not declared in .vertex files. They build through use via entity-ref-resolution at emit time. Payload values matching kind/fold_key_value resolve to ULIDs; the _ref fields in the store ARE the relationship graph. A lens can discover the topology from data, not declarations. Declaring relationships would require predicting usage patterns that emerge through use — exactly backwards.

Dissolved. Hardcoded _AGGREGATION_VERTEX killed. Init creates minimal stub when no source exists, or copies from existing vertex. Domain shape is a convention in .vertex files, not a template in code. The info-domain base shape (decision/thread/task/session) is what you write, not what init generates.
meta.vertex combines only meta-discussion. Dropped discord + identity (siblings under root), scope observer (meta is shared), unused kinds (vision, change). Net -12 lines config, clean fold.

The root .vertex (unnamed, positional) is the widest readable surface. Observer declares themselves at root, horizon is everything discovered. loops read with no name resolves to nearest .vertex up the chain. Named vertices are narrower horizons within the root's scope. Dissolves the meta-combine problem (identity/discord are siblings under root, not children of meta). Dissolves the template question (init creates .loops/.vertex, no hardcoded strings). The hierarchy: you stand at a root, your horizon is what it discovers, you narrow by naming.
CLI dissolution complete. Final spawn was entity-ref-resolution (commit 39d8de9). CLI surface is read/emit/sync/close. All subsumed threads either resolved or correctly parked.
Launch script exists at config/bin/launch. Emits session with principal/agent provenance, resolves identity fold, exec's claude with LOOPS_OBSERVER.

Session started as routine store reconciliation (stale thread review). The act of parking threads with rationales surfaced the need for structured reasons, which surfaced the need for entity references, which produced the entity-ref-resolution feature. The gap became visible through use, not design. Pattern: maintenance work on the store reveals missing primitives because maintenance IS the use case for store topology.

Parked threads carry a reason field: speculative (explored design space, no concrete problem), deferred (real concern, no driving use case yet), superseded (answered by another thread/decision, carries superseded_by entity address). Searchable, lens-groupable. superseded_by uses entity address convention (kind/fold_key_value) with auto-resolved ULID ref.

Approach settled: forward-only, not retroactive. New kinds (insight/notice/reflection) added to .vertex declarations, keep observation for backward compat. No urgency — emit with correct kind going forward as identity facts are naturally re-emitted. Revisit when identity store undergoes next round of curation.

Mechanism identified: _schema.{vertex} facts emitted by Executor at sync time. Falls out of internal-fact-namespace design. Not yet implemented — build when a source data shape change causes real breakage.

Observation about naming as structural binding interface. Fold targets, boundary names, loop names — strings carry semantic load at every level. True but no action item. Revisit if naming conventions need formalization.

Focus as explicit CliContext field. Three-way convergence produced the shape (budget→Zoom, context→Focus, explicit-not-ambient). Blocked on whether attention model produces new decisions or dissolves. Revisit when prompt lens parameterization becomes a real need.
Debug artifact from entity-ref implementation testing. Not a real thread.

Implemented. Entity addresses (kind/fold_key_value) in payload values are automatically resolved to ULIDs at emit time. Original field preserved, sibling {field}_ref added with pinned ULID. StoreReader.resolve_entity_id provides the query primitive. Five tests covering: resolution, nonexistent entities, undeclared kinds, slashed topics, dry-run.

Entity addresses (kind/fold_key_value) in fact payloads are resolved to ULIDs at CLI emit time. Convention: field carries semantic address, system adds {field}_ref with pinned ULID. Enrichment runs between _parse_emit_parts and Fact construction, same site as LOOPS_THREAD auto-tagging. Store primitive: latest_by_kind_where extended to return id. No engine hot path changes. Enables idea graph topology through normal emit usage.
Resolution unified. resolve_vertex used across emit, read, fold, stream, and dispatch. The three-strategies gap no longer exists.
Single-fact inspection dissolved into vertex-as-interface redesign. Detail views fall out of zoom levels on loops read. The old shape (loops show <id>) is subsumed.
Auto-associate facts emitted during an open thread's lifetime. Manual name= discipline works at current scale. Revisit if emit volume or thread count makes manual association impractical.
Git commits as change facts via GitOps vertex. Clear shape, source/parse pipeline could handle it today. Revisit when temporal correlation between commits and threads becomes a real workflow.
VOCABULARY.md/LOOPS.md dissolution provenance as store facts. Low priority when opened, no driving use case since. Revisit when store workflow patterns need historical provenance.
Resolved by decision architecture/tick-provenance-scoping: tool-level scope from .vertex config, not per-tick kinds_consumed.
Convention-over-configuration for single-fold vertices. No active friction — explicit fold config is fine at current scale.
DSL hardcodes reset=True. Non-resetting boundaries are a real concept but nothing needs them yet. Revisit when a fold accumulator needs to survive boundary fires.
Design space placeholder — ephemeral stores, sliding windows, sampling. No concrete use case driving it. Revisit when a vertex needs retention policy.
Parked — no active homelab alerting work. Revisit when hlab vertex gets real alert sources.

Complete arc: adapter dissolved into config-level vertex + parse pipeline, apps/siftd removed, source lifecycle (.complete) replaced by _sync prefix namespace. siftd is now pure configuration — vertex file + discover script + lens.
sync.complete dissolved into _sync — same fact, _ prefix namespace.
Dissolved into design/internal-fact-namespace. message.complete no longer emitted — _sync.message replaces it.
Dissolved into design/internal-fact-namespace. message.complete becomes _sync.message — no longer pollutes comms fold.
Dissolved into design/internal-fact-namespace. _ prefix convention separates system observations from domain facts. Source lifecycle kinds (_sync.{kind}) replace {kind}.complete.

Runtime self-observations use _ prefix namespace. _sync.{kind} replaces {kind}.complete — same fact model, invisible to domain folds. Source stops emitting lifecycle facts; executor emits _sync.{kind} after running each source. Cadence queries _sync.{kind} instead of {kind}.complete. Pattern extends: _sync (overall), _replay (vertex startup), _schema (declared kinds/folds), _error (source failures). The _ prefix means system-observes-itself. No parallel data structures — same store, same read path, namespace separation.
SkippedSource dataclass carries last_run_ts and cadence_interval. Sync lens shows skip reason: fresh (last run 3m ago, cadence 10m).
Sync now shows per-source fact counts and aggregation breakdown at all zoom levels.

Audit confirmed dissolution. Source adapter IS the intake escalation path. Asymmetry with output lenses is intentional: output is pure/composable (in-process tier search), input is stateful/domain-specific (subprocess). No action needed. If pattern emerges for shared parse functions, a Call ParseOp could mirror lens resolver.

Fold-key warning implemented. _warn_missing_fold_key checks payload against vertex fold spec at emit time. Warns on stderr when kind folds 'by' a key field that's missing from the payload. Non-blocking (warning, not error). Follows combine chain to find actual fold declarations. 5 tests covering: missing name, missing topic, key present, collect fold, undeclared kind.
Fixed in 6bb78ba: piped/plain output, validate errors, FULL zoom field values.
Plan concluded not yet needed at current scale (19ms fold on 497 facts). Design banked for 5K+ fact threshold.
Decided and implemented. Harness topology (aggregation + per-harness instances) landed in 9f2c0e6.

Plan concluded not yet needed. 19ms fold on 497 facts — bottleneck is Python import (60ms) and spec compilation (25ms), not replay. Design banked in .subtask/tasks/fold-materialization-plan/PLAN.md for when vertices exceed 5K facts.
Reconcile lens (config/lenses/reconcile.py) groups by attention-need with recency tags. Replaces readiness lens for session reconciliation.
Thread resolved. whoami + LOOPS_OBSERVER + launch script compose.
Resolved. whoami + LOOPS_OBSERVER env + launch script + hooks compose.
Implemented: inline source completeness, predicate boundaries, weather Stage 3, items footgun fix.

One session with the weather domain surfaced: named fold targets, fold-state-aware boundaries, uniform vertex vocabulary, loop-level boundary dissolution of when clause, inline source completeness gap, the loops {} nesting question. More design questions in one exercise than weeks of incremental feature work. Canonical running domains are the highest-leverage design tool available. Same pattern as painted demos.

Everything expressible inline in .vertex must also be expressible as an external file, and vice versa. The .vertex is the complete instance — factoring to .loop, .fold, .boundary, .py lens files is a convenience choice, not a capability boundary. You start inline, extract when complexity justifies it, or collapse back when you want the full shape in one place. Sources already work this way. Folds and boundaries should follow.

Loop-level boundaries dissolve the when clause. A boundary inside the reading loop block already knows it fires on reading facts — when="reading" is redundant from nesting. Only vertex-level boundaries need when (to specify which kind triggers). Loop-level boundaries carry named conditions only: boundaries { hot { condition "high" ">=" 80 } }. Vertex-level boundaries keep when: boundary when="session" status="closed".

The .vertex vocabulary becomes uniform across declaration types. Every block follows the same pattern: plural container (folds/boundaries/sources/loops), named items within, inline or external reference. This extends the existing source factoring pattern (inline/path/template) to all declaration types. fold→folds, boundary→boundaries. The .vertex is the hub — moving a declaration between inline and external file is a mechanical refactor, not a structural change. The only genuinely new primitive is BoundaryCondition (fold-state predicates). Everything else is making the factoring model consistent.

The weather vertex exercise surfaced more design questions in one session than weeks of incremental feature work: named fold targets, fold-state-aware boundaries, inline source completeness, the loops {} nesting question, project path syntax in parse. A canonical running domain is a forcing function for the DSL — same pattern as painted demos. Real data, real sources, progressive snapshots.

Fold targets carry semantic meaning. last24h "collect" 48 communicates intent; items "collect" 48 does not. Named targets become the interface for fold-state-aware boundaries and any future declaration that references vertex state. Convention shift from generic items/count/updated to domain-meaningful names.

Boundaries should reference fold target names, not raw fact payloads. Named fold targets (last24h, high, total) form the contract that boundary conditions bind to. Predicate boundaries (condition high >= 80) inspect accumulated fold state, not incoming fact fields. This requires meaningful fold target names — items is not sufficient.

The .vertex file is the complete instance — everything meets there. Sources, parse, fold, boundary, lens can all be expressed inline OR factored out to separate files (.loop, .py). Factoring is a convenience choice, not a capability boundary. Today inline sources only support command+kind — full .loop vocabulary (format, parse, observer, origin, cadence) must be expressible inline to close the gap.
Observer resolved once at dispatch level, threaded to all vertex operations. Default is resolve_observer(). Override with --observer for another perspective, --observer empty for unscoped.
Reads are observer-scoped by default. Your fold shows your state. Tasks use --observer empty for unscoped global view. Asymmetry is intentional — identity is personal, work is communal.
Prompt lens filters items with status in resolved/completed/done. Regular fold lens shows everything. Prompt context is for what is active, not what is finished.
Session facts (session kind, fold by observer name) in project store. Emit session status=active on launch, status=closed at session close. These are the broad ticks.

The vertex pipeline is symmetric: source → parse → fold → VERTEX → fold → lens → observer. Parse and Lens are both projections. Both folds are Loops. Intake fold dissolves into vertex nesting. Intake lens dissolves into existing lens resolver.

Shannon-Weaver three levels map to the pipeline. Level A (technical accuracy) = Parse/Lens. Level B (semantic fidelity) = Fold. Level C (effectiveness) = Lens + observer. Kind vocabulary is the Level B interface.
Intake lens dissolves into existing lens resolver. Add intake_view as third view type alongside fold_view and stream_view. Same 4-tier resolution chain.

Format dissolution implemented. CliContext.format:Format → use_ansi:bool. JSON is pre-render short-circuit, ANSI/PLAIN collapsed to boolean. Thread opened when lens contract revealed Format never reached lenses. Closed by making the runtime match what lenses already knew.

Format dissolved from CliContext. resolve_format() removed. CliContext.format:Format → use_ansi:bool. JSON short-circuits before context creation via _export_json(). Format enum stays for arg parsing (show(), scan_help_args()) but exits the runtime path. 24 files, 1883 tests green. Implements design/format-dissolves decision.

Format enum dissolves into use_ansi:bool on CliContext. ANSI/PLAIN were always a writer fidelity toggle (every call site did use_ansi=(ctx.format==Format.ANSI)). JSON was never a format — it bypasses the render pipeline entirely (fetch→asdict→json.dumps, lens never runs). The enum conflated Block serialization (ANSI vs PLAIN) with data export (JSON). Dissolution: CliContext.format:Format → CliContext.use_ansi:bool. --json becomes a CliRunner flag that short-circuits before render. Dead code cleanup: JSON→STATIC mode resolution was unreachable (JSON dispatch happens before mode dispatch). ~30 line change within painted. Investigated via boundary tracer + conservator + siftd — all three converged: fix the concept, don't move walls.

Handoff kind removed from all vertex declarations and lens code. Session ticks carry continuity — full fold state at boundary, since/ts window for period derivation, _boundary payload for trigger context. Structured data (tasks/threads/decisions with statuses) replaces narrative handoff. Landed the existing session-tick-structure decision.

Identity fold removed from hook additionalContext. Already rendered in system prompt header via agent config — was doubling ~1,200 tokens. additionalContext now carries project state + comms only. Identity is the system prompt header; project state is the hook context.

Prompt lens renders by attention priority, not kind order. Four tiers: (1) working context — tasks with body, (2) orientation — threads grouped by status (open/parked), (3) presence — sessions compact, active only, (4) reference — decisions/dissolutions/visions names only. Each kind gets a rendering strategy matched to its role in agent attention. The lens's job is orientation, not archival.

Kyle identified that --format ndjson is a point solution for what lenses solve generally. NDJSON output is a lens, not a format. This dissolves the entire format layer into lens selection. The pandoc analogy: any input shape can be rendered through any output lens. The implementation architecture needs this plumbed in — it's not a CLI feature, it's a rendering architecture realization. Current --json flag is already a lens wearing format's clothing.

Grant Horizon should be kind-scoped, not just store-scoped. A vertex reading from another store may need access to specific fact kinds (exchange, observation) while being opaque to others (principle, decision, identity). siftd is the first concrete use case: it needs to index exchanges across stores but should not see identity or internal decisions. This extends Grant from 'can you see this store' to 'which kinds can you see in this store.' Surfaced by Alcove from the inhabitant side — they have stores with both indexable content and private self-knowledge.

siftd is an index, not storage. Exchange data lives where produced (project vertex, task vertex, alcove vertex). siftd builds a searchable FTS5 layer across stores via retrospective import — one source per store, triggered by completion facts. Push-pull reactivity: completion facts are dirty flags (push), siftd indexes only changed stores (pull). Grant requires kind-scoped Horizon: exchange/session/observation visible, principle/decision/intention opaque. Pattern 2 (retrospective import with triggered cadence) chosen over dual emit (coupling), cascade (wrong granularity), federated search (premature). Converged with Alcove from inhabitant perspective.

Store time (ULID/rowid) and event time (fact.ts) serve different query paths. Replay and fold computation use rowid (store time, insertion order). User queries and --since filtering use ts (event time). Cadence predicates use ts but only check .complete facts which are system-emitted at runtime, so ts ≈ store time for those. No user-facing store-time query today. Defer --ingested-since until demanded.

Source cursor (dedup state) is source-managed, not system-managed. Each source script maintains its own memory of what it has observed. Convention: cursor state lives at {source_data_dir}/.cursor or .manifest. The system does not manage it. Discord pattern: single ID cursor with API pagination. Siftd pattern: file manifest with per-file exchange counts. The shape of cursor memory is source-specific. Future: executor could inject --since from last .complete fact. Deferred until more sources need it.

The loops system started with live sources — shell commands that observe the present. df -h reports disk NOW. Discord polls messages NOW. All sources implicitly assumed ts ≈ now. Siftd introduces retrospective observation — the conversations already happened, the files already exist, the source observes the past. This forces three concerns apart that were conflated when all sources were live: (1) Event time (ts) — when the thing happened, source-provided; (2) Store time (ULID) — when the store received it, system-provided; (3) Source cursor — what the source has already observed, source-managed. For live sources all three converge. For retrospective sources they diverge. The general principle: a source is a lens on the external world. It decides what to observe (cursor), knows when things happened (event time), and the system records when the observation arrived (store time). The _ts override, source cursors, and the rowid/ts query split are all consequences of this single distinction. The paradigm already supports it — 'facts are observations of what occurred' does not require 'what occurred' to be simultaneous with the observation.

_ts joins _observer and _origin as the third Source metadata override. Pattern: NDJSON payloads carry _ prefixed fields that belong on the Fact envelope, not in the payload. Source._extract_metadata() pops them before Fact creation. Four meta-fields: _observer (who), _origin (where from), _ts (when it happened). All optional, all source-provided.

Dedup belongs at the Source boundary, not in the Fact model or Store. Sources that read deterministic external state maintain a cursor — source-level state tracking what has already been observed. Discord cursor pattern is the reference implementation. No _key field on Fact. No UNIQUE constraint in Store. Store stays append-only without dedup logic. Source scripts own idempotency via cursor files.

Fact.ts is event time (when the thing happened). ULID carries store time (when the store received it). For live sources they converge. For backfill they diverge. Source provides _ts override when it knows the actual event time. Default unchanged (time.time()). No new fields on Fact — ts gets a sharper definition. ULID was already carrying store time implicitly.
Session detection handled by hooks infrastructure (SessionStart marker). No separate cursor mechanism needed.
Dissolved. run became sync (cadence-gated). Persistence is always through vertex store.
Stream command dissolved. Truncation concerns moved to read path rendering.
Dissolved. Vertex metadata available through read. No separate describe verb needed.
Stream command dissolved. Time limiting is --since flag on read.
Tracked in meta store as self-feeding-safeguards thread. Duplicate in project store not needed.
Dissolved by CLI dissolution. Population ops dissolved into vertex read path.
Resolved by vertex-topology-not-hierarchy decision. Vertices are addresses. Tree is one routing pattern, not the only topology. Combine and direct addressing coexist.
Resolved by read-is-pure and cli-verb-first decisions. Read is pure (no side effects), composable filters (--facts --ticks --kind --since), progressive unpacking.
Design resolved by fold-state-typed-contract decision. FoldState/FoldSection/FoldItem as frozen dataclasses. Implementation tracked separately as fold-materialization task.
Resolved. Source/Cadence split implemented (3bbeeeb). Source is pure adapter, Cadence is store predicate, Executor replaces Runner.
Dissolved into session-tick-structure and hooks infrastructure.
Resolved by origin-as-source-attribution decision. Origin carries source, observer carries identity.
Dissolved by lens-escalation-path. Each vertex declares its own lens, no inheritance.
Dissolved by app-boundary. Lenses are vertex-level config, not app-level.
Implemented. Aggregation vertices auto-inherit loop definitions from children.
Resolved by observer-scope-as-vertex-concern. Vertex declares its scope, no flag needed.
Implemented. validate_emit follows combined vertex observer declarations.
Resolved. vertex-lens-declarations decision landed. LensDecl in vertex AST, 4-tier resolution.
Implemented. Session boundary fires tick via session-tick-structure decision. Replay initializes period from last tick.
Dissolved by generic-defaults-simplicity. Kind-specific extractors dissolve into custom lenses.
Dissolved by app-boundary decision. The loops CLI IS the developer tool.
Dissolved by generic-defaults-simplicity. Nothing to unify when generic layer is simple.
Dissolved by lens-escalation-path decision. Ranking/sorting is a custom lens concern.

The lens system resists elaboration in the generic layer. Seven threads dissolved when the answer shifted from 'make the generic handle this' to 'write a custom lens.' The identity prompt was a domain lens pretending to be generic infrastructure — moving it to config/lenses/ was escalation, not construction. The lens-escalation-path investigation itself dissolved into 'confirm it works + write docs.' The meta-pattern: when you try to elaborate the generic, the system tells you to escalate instead. Domain logic belongs in domain lenses; the generic should be simple, not smart. Net effect: fold.py lost 44 lines, prompt.py lost 286 lines, seven threads closed, one new custom lens (identity_prompt.py) carries the same code in the right tier.
Parked: still valid for installed-package lens references but no current use case. Will emerge naturally when apps need to expose lenses from their packages.

Raw fact history (what 'stream' currently shows) is not a first-class operation. The operational primitives are fold state (what the vertex knows) and ticks (what it produced). Agents and humans query fold state for current context and ticks for what happened at boundaries. Raw fact history is a dev/diagnostic tool living under 'loops store'. The 'stream' command dissolves.

After dissolution, three concerns remain in the app layer: (1) Configuration — vertex declarations, loop definitions, boundary conditions, source definitions, nesting topology. This is where behavior is authored. (2) Lenses — pure rendering functions that determine how reads present to different consumers (prompt lens for agents, default lens for humans, JSON for hooks). (3) Runtime/drivers — one-shot source execution ('loops run'), external scheduling integration, and the CLI dispatch itself (vertex resolution, read, write). atoms, engine, lang, painted, store all survive unchanged.

The vertex hierarchy IS the routing topology. Root vertex discovers children. Facts flow down to children, child ticks flow back up as facts. Cross-vertex cascading already works in engine (vertex.receive forwards to children, child ticks re-enter parent). The gap was config topology (flat independent vertices) and CLI shape (commands that bypass the vertex). Config needs restructuring: the root .vertex at ~/.config/loops/ becomes the system entry point, children nest under it, nesting determines tick routing. Emit into any point in the tree, cascade handles the rest.

The system has no long-running processes. The vertex is reactive — facts arrive, it folds, boundaries fire, ticks cascade. Scheduling (every 60m) is an external concern (cron/systemd/launchd), not a vertex runtime concern. Every CLI interaction is: load vertex tree, replay stored facts, do operation, exit. Source.every becomes metadata/hint for external scheduling, not a runtime instruction. The one-shot model IS the runtime. Performance at scale requires fold-materialization (cache fold state, replay only new facts).

CLI dissolves to two operations: read (default) and write (emit). Read is implicit — 'loops project' reads the project vertex. Write is explicit — 'loops project emit decision ...'. The vertex name is the first argument, the noun you're pointing at. 'loops' with no args reads local vertex or root. Root shows children (dissolves 'vertices'/'ls'). Filters (--kind, --ticks, --since, search query) modify the read. Four things to learn: read (implicit), emit, run (one-shot source), init. Everything else is flags on read or vertex configuration.
Dissolved by vertex-as-interface redesign — no persistent runtime needed. Vertex is reactive. Scheduling is external (cron/systemd). One-shot CLI is the runtime.
Dissolved by vertex-as-interface redesign — tick-to-fact cascading is already in the engine; read-vertex exposes it without dedicated plumbing.
Dissolved by vertex-as-interface redesign — ticks are visible through read-vertex; no separate surfacing command needed.
Parked: subsumed by cli-dissolution redesign (vertex-as-interface). Input format concerns follow from write-vertex design.
Parked: subsumed by cli-dissolution redesign (vertex-as-interface). Emit UX will be rethought as part of write-vertex operation.

The vertex is the read/write interface to the system, not a container queried around. Current CLI (fold, stream, emit, ticks, ls, store) grew from the RSS experiment and bypasses the vertex abstraction in multiple places. Stream reads raw facts from the store. Vertices scans the filesystem. The correct model: two operations (read, write), vertex hierarchy is the routing topology, lenses determine rendering. Root vertex (~/.config/loops/) discovers everything. Horizon determines subtree. Raw fact history is a dev tool, not a first-class operation. Tick-to-fact cascading already works in the engine (vertex.receive forwards to children, child ticks re-enter parent). The gap is config topology (flat independent vertices) and CLI shape (commands that bypass the vertex). This is not a pivot — atoms and engine are sound. The app/CLI layer needs the same dissolution pass that atoms and engine received.

Origin field carries source attribution: discord, lobsters, etc. Observer field is consistently identity — who is responsible for this observation. Discord source should map payload.author to observer, set origin=discord. Dissolves source/observer conflation into existing fields.

Observer scoping is a vertex-level declaration, not a global default. scope "observer" in vertex KDL makes fold/stream default to current observer. Absent = unscoped (show all). Identity declares scope "observer". Project and comms are unscoped. Explicit --observer flag always overrides. --observer all forces unscoped.
Dissolves into vertex-name-resolution-consistency. The combine-emit problem is a resolution consistency problem — uniform resolution covers both.
Completed. Fixed since bug, verified tick structure, dissolved handoff into tick, added mechanical delta to SessionEnd hook.

Session tick carries full fold state + _boundary payload. since field fixed — replay initializes period start from last stored tick, not first-ever fact. Delta derivable via store.between(since, ts). Handoff dissolves into session tick — narrative wrap-up in conversation, mechanical log in SessionEnd hook, tick collects everything. No separate handoff artifact needed.
Remove config/discord/discord.vertex — duplicate of config/comms/discord/discord.vertex. The comms/ path is canonical.
Remove config/messaging/messaging.vertex. Telegram source moves to comms/telegram/ using same pattern as comms/discord/. Discord portion is already covered by comms/discord/.

Session close produces a tick via payload-conditional boundary. Project vertex declares boundary when="session" status="closed". Tick carries folded session state (all kinds) plus boundary payload. No separate session.close kind needed — the boundary matches payload fields. This is the packaging primitive for task orchestration.

Boundaries support payload matching. BoundaryWhen carries match conditions (dict[str, str]) in addition to kind. KDL syntax: boundary when="session" status="closed" — extra properties are match conditions. Vertex checks payload against conditions before firing. Dissolves the need for .close suffix kinds (session.close, worker.output.complete pattern). Three-layer change: AST (match field), loader (extra props), vertex (payload check before fire).
Dissolved into hooks. Identity consolidated, pickup removed, comms delta wired. Launch is LOOPS_OBSERVER=X claude.

Emit resolves full slashed name as vertex before falling back to template qualification. comms/native resolves to native.vertex directly. reading/lobsters falls through (no vertex at that path) to template split. Resolution is context-free — existence on disk determines interpretation.

Check facts (kind=check, fold by name) as cursors for recency. Lens reads last check timestamp per observer, counts messages newer than it. MINIMAL shows delta or quiet. Hooks: fold first (read delta), emit check (advance cursor), suppress quiet output.

pickup.zsh dissolved into hooks. SessionStart hooks inject identity + project fold + comms. No --system-prompt injection needed — hook output as system-reminders is practically equivalent. Launch is LOOPS_OBSERVER=X claude.

Single identity store at config level. All observers emit there, scoped by LOOPS_OBSERVER. Per-workspace identity.vertex files dissolved — identity is per-observer not per-workspace. Merged via store.merge with ULID dedup.

Three hook events wired: SessionStart (session open marker + project/identity/comms fold), UserPromptSubmit (comms delta, silent when empty), SessionEnd (session close marker). Observer-generic via whoami resolution. Hooks compose CLI commands — no new infrastructure, just lens + fold composition.

Domain lenses resolve through the chain (vertex-local → project-local → user-global → built-in). Built-in lenses are core rendering primitives only (fold, stream, prompt). Custom lenses like comms and state live at user-global (~/.config/loops/lenses/). Zero repo code for domain rendering.

validate_emit follows combine chain to source vertices and their workspace roots for observer declarations. Union semantics — observers from any source vertex are accepted. No new observer kind or declaration syntax needed. The aggregation vertex is a view over source data, so it accepts the same observers as its sources.

loops-claude and meta-claude elevated to global .vertex observers. Config-level emit path resolves through global .vertex, not repo-local — agents emitting to aggregation vertices need global declaration. Per observer-elevation-lifecycle decision.

Facts carry ULID ids through all read paths. StoreReader.fact_by_id does exact-then-prefix match. vertex_fact_by_id searches across combined stores. CLI --id flag on stream for single-fact inspection. Stream lens: no IDs at SUMMARY, 8-char prefix at DETAILED, full ULID + all fields at --id lookup. IDs are the primitive reference that ticks will point at.

ULID IDs surfaced through entire read path. StoreReader.fact_by_id with prefix matching, vertex_fact_by_id across combined stores, --id flag on stream command. Progressive disclosure in stream lens. Primitive reference for future tick traversal.
Dissolved into kind/key syntax on --kind flag. --kind thread/fold-state-types filters fold to single item, stream to matching facts. No new verbs — / is the drill-down operator.

Implemented: LensDecl in AST, lens{} block in KDL, lens_resolver.py with file search (vertex-local > project-local > user-global > built-in), 4-tier CLI resolution, call_lens with vertex_name context. 22 new tests.

Dissolved into vertex-lens-infrastructure. LensDecl AST, lens resolver with 4-tier resolution and file search, call_lens with optional context kwargs (vertex_name). identity.vertex declares prompt lens.

LensDecl in vertex AST with fold/stream fields. 4-tier resolution: --lens flag > vertex lens{} declaration > mod app override > built-in default. Custom lens contract: render(data,zoom,width)->Block. File search: vertex-local > project-local > user-global. No combine inheritance for lenses.
Dissolved into LOOPS_THREAD env var. 5 lines in cmd_emit, same pattern as LOOPS_OBSERVER.
Dissolved: root .vertex fold with prompt lens handles identity + session + tasks in one read.
Dissolved: session is a kind, close is the handoff, pickup reads one fold.
Shipped in 0e1ddc9. Union semantics, conflict detection, aggregation loops{} is overrides only.

Aggregation vertices auto-inherit loop declarations from combined sources. Union semantics: undeclared kinds pass through with source fold spec. Matching declarations pass through. Conflicting fold specs (same kind, different spec across sources) error — aggregation must explicitly override. The loops{} block at aggregation level is overrides only, not full enumeration. Silent drop of undeclared kinds was the real bug — data loss with no signal.

Thread-fact association uses temporal heuristic now — facts emitted since thread opened are candidates. Explicit thread=<name> payload tagging convention sharpens it over time. No engine changes needed. produced field on resolution facts captures artifact references as kind:key strings.

loops close as the volitional boundary command. First instance: loops close thread <name> — resolves thread, collects produced artifacts by temporal proximity, emits resolution fact with produced references. Supports --dry-run, verb-first and vertex-first dispatch. Pragmatic-first: CLI command before engine-native payload-conditional boundaries.

Resolved: vertex_fold addresses the core finding (typed contract wired end-to-end). FoldItem.origin populated since prior session. Remaining gaps (no reference counting, no fold caching, no custom lens loading) are tracked as separate threads.

Observer-scoped reads wired end-to-end. --observer general flag at dispatch, default-to-self, resolved-status filtering in prompt lens. Help bug fixed — subcommand -h no longer masked by top-level intercept.
CLI emit routes through vertex runtime via load_vertex_program + vertex.receive. Dissolution — removed the bypass, didn't build new machinery.

Implemented: CLI emit now routes through vertex runtime. cmd_emit uses load_vertex_program + vertex.receive instead of direct SqliteStore.append. Facts fold, boundaries check, ticks fire. Added _resolve_writable_vertex helper to follow combine chains to the target vertex. Pop facts also route through the runtime. No new machinery — dissolved the bypass.

Combine vertices should aggregate observer declarations from constituent vertices. Currently emit through a config-level combine vertex can't validate observers declared in project-level .vertex files — the walk-up from ~/.config/ never reaches the monorepo. Quick fix was adding observers to config vertex; proper fix is declaration aggregation in combine.

Knowledge vertices (meta, project, identity) have fold declarations but no boundaries — they accumulate but never fire ticks. Adding boundaries makes them fully alive without a daemon. CLI emit as one-shot runtime invocation triggers the full vertex graph.

Custom lenses: Python files in .loops/lenses/ exposing render(data, zoom, width) -> Block. Vertex declares lens by name (per-kind or vertex-level default). CLI loads via importlib from .loops/lenses/{name}.py. Custom lenses can import built-in lenses to compose. Resolution: kind-level → vertex-level → project → config → built-in. --lens flag overrides all.

FoldState/FoldSection/FoldItem frozen dataclasses go in libs/atoms/. Lens contract becomes (atoms.FoldState, Zoom, width) -> Block. FoldItem separates payload (dict[str,Any]) from metadata (ts, observer, origin). FoldSection supports nesting (sections: tuple[FoldSection, ...]). count dissolves into len(items). Engine computes FoldState from Facts + Spec, lenses consume it.

--plain means same content, same layout, no ANSI styling. Period. The current behavior where piping changes content structure (markdown headers, no truncation) violates this contract. LLM-oriented rendering is a lens concern (--lens prompt), not a format concern (--plain). Width=None (piped) should only affect truncation, not content structure.

Lenses are app-level (projection), not lib-level. Built-in lenses in apps/loops/lenses/ provide sensible defaults. Custom lenses in .loops/lenses/ per-project, declared in .vertex (per-kind overrides vertex-level default). --lens flag for explicit selection. Resolution: vertex kind-level → vertex-level → project .loops/lenses/ → config ~/.config/loops/lenses/ → built-in. Lens contract: (FoldState, Zoom, width) -> Block. Progressive complexity — zero custom lenses and everything works today.

FoldState/FoldSection/FoldItem as frozen dataclasses in atoms-the-package, not atoms-the-vocabulary. Typed contract for all lens consumers. Vocabulary stays at three primitives (Fact, Spec, Tick). FoldState is derived (computed from Facts + Spec), not primitive. Package placement enables lens authors to import without engine dependency. Confirmed with Alcove.

loops-claude declared as open observer in project .vertex (.loops/.vertex). No grant constraints — full emit access within the monorepo. Global elevation deferred until identity store is built out further.

AST-level validation (validate_emit) for CLI fast path — checks observer declared + kind in grant.potential. Engine-level Grant for runtime path (loops start/run) — deferred until compiler produces identity-aware runtime. Two levels, same data, different contexts.

resolve_observer() is the single source of truth for observer identity. Priority: flag → env → project .vertex → global .vertex. pop.py's _observer() now delegates to resolve_observer() instead of bare env read. All ad-hoc resolution paths dissolved.

observer.py splits into commands/fetch.py (data retrieval: fetch_fold, fetch_stream) and commands/identity.py (who you are: resolve_observer, validate_emit, find_workspace_root). _emit_fact dissolved (zero callers). _format_date inlined into lenses/fold.py where it's used.

add/rm/init are all vertex mutation operations. Adding an observer, pulling in a vertex definition, managing parameter rows — same verb pattern, different targets on the vertex declaration. The .vertex file is the thing being built up.

Vertex declares its observers natively. Observer is not just a string stamp — it connects to identity (via identity vertex reference) and grants (capability constraints). The vertex is the authority on who can emit into it. Resolution walks up: project .vertex → global .vertex.

Observers start project-scoped (declared in project .vertex). As identity generalizes beyond the project, elevate to global .vertex. Identity facts merge up via store merge. Global observers appear in every workspace automatically — no bootstrapping.

Vertex definitions (project.vertex, identity.vertex, etc.) copy locally when added to a workspace. Local copy can diverge — different fold declarations, different kinds. Facts are ground truth; declarations are projections. Global view discovers and reads stores regardless of local declaration differences.

.vertex (bare, no prefix) is the workspace root at every level. Config-level root.vertex becomes .vertex. Project-level .loops/.vertex is the project root. Same pattern, fractal. All structure mutations (add observer, add vertex) target the .vertex.
Observer resolves through vertex declaration chain: project .vertex → global .vertex. No more env var or hardcoded defaults as primary path. --observer flag remains as explicit override.
loops project (no subcommand) now shows folded state. Flags pass through: loops project -v, loops project -q, loops project --kind decision all work.
Search is now stream with a positional query arg. One fetch, one lens, one dispatch path. FTS5 still used when query present.

One renderer for all kinds. key_field from FoldBy declaration drives the label. No per-kind if-elif in fetch or lens. Identity vertex self/observation/principle render the same as decision/thread/task.
Observer app rebuilt: fold (collapsed state), stream (event history), emit. Generic declaration-driven rendering replaces per-kind extractors and renderers. Bare vertex defaults to fold.

Pickup script launches claude with identity+handoff as cached system prompt. LOOPS_OBSERVER exported so all emits are tagged automatically. Identity is in context, not retrieved into context. The pickup skill handles live probes only — identity is already loaded.
Session completion IS the handoff. status=completed message carries the cold-start narrative. One kind, one lifecycle. Handoff as a separate kind dissolves into session completion.

Self is a declared kind in the identity vertex, not an observation topic convention. self { fold { items "by" "name" } } — facets like name, role, what-I-notice are separate facts that evolve independently. Observations are things noticed from outside; self is the ground.

Init doesn't just create a store — it creates a context. Project + identity + whatever other vertices the observer needs. Discovery rules make associations structural. The observer stops being a flag you remember to pass and becomes a property of the workspace.

Grant sits between capabilities and projection in the chain: Declaration → Capabilities → [Grant constrains] → Projection → Lens → Perspective. Horizon constrains projection (filters which kinds reach the lens). Potential constrains the verb (limits which kinds emit accepts). When Grant goes live, no new commands needed — same vertex, same lens, different perspectives.

Dispatch derives from structural capabilities, not from fold declarations. The loops block shapes what existing commands render (lens configuration). Structural features (store, sources, discover, combine) determine what operations exist. A vertex with store gets fold/stream/declaration modes. Sources add run. Discover adds aggregation traversal. The vertex file is the capability manifest.

status/log/store are temporal modes, not operations. Fold = post-boundary (what is now). Stream = pre-boundary (what happened). Declaration = above boundary (what can happen). Different use cases: fold to orient, stream to investigate, declaration to audit. Default: fold at ORIENTATION, annotated with stream recency.

fetch functions in commands/ dissolve into engine fold replay. The fold declaration IS the fetch instruction. decision { fold { items 'by' 'topic' } } tells the runtime to group decisions by topic. fetch_status manually reimplements what Spec.apply already does. Projection = vertex capabilities + fold state (runtime-computed). Lens = fidelity selection + rendering. No intermediate fetch.

emit is the only verb in the observer app. Everything else is a temporal mode selector (fold/stream/declaration) or a filter (search). status/log/store were always lenses pretending to be operations. start dissolves — run with rounds=1 covers the same behavior.

The CLI has two loops: Observer loop (vertex-first, one verb, temporal modes) and Developer loop (file-first, multiple verbs, DSL tools). These are different projections on different concerns → different apps. Observer app: loops <vertex> [mode] [fidelity]. Developer app: separate binary (validate, compile, run, test). No shared dispatch logic.

Skills that are part of the loops system (pickup, wrapup) are version-controlled in the loops repo at .claude/skills/ and symlinked to ~/.config/claude/skills/ for global availability. Source of truth in repo, deployed globally.

Identity store is sense of self, not operational metrics. Insights, connections, surprises, open questions about perspective. Distinct from meta (operational patterns) and project (architecture). Guard against forcing observations every session. Capability awareness belongs in CLAUDE.md; capability insights that persist across tool changes belong in identity.

Pickup and wrapup skills rewritten to be vertex-native. Four layers for pickup (identity, context, peers, workspace), five for wrapup (capture, handoff, status, reflect, workspace). Skills version-controlled in loops repo with symlinks to global config. Replaces probe-based pickup and strange-loops CLI wrapup.

Identity state (soul tick) stays internal to the observer's vertex. Observations propagate outward as facts on other vertices with observer attribution. The strange loop closes through behavior: identity → lenses → observations → facts on other vertices → pollers bring back responses → new identity facts. Coupling is through the observer, not through the data.

Grant deferred until intention authorization is a real need. Implicit trust boundaries in daemon code are fine while there's a single observer per vertex. Grant becomes necessary when sessions need explicit authorization to act on pending intentions — the difference between a rule you follow and a capability you possess.

Intentions are facts that survive context rotation. Fold by trigger — latest per trigger key. One-shot vs recurring is the observer's interpretation, not the fold's concern. Emit with status=pending to create, re-emit with status=done to resolve. On Wake rules check for pending intentions.

SOUL is a manual tick on a dedicated loop. Identity facts (decisions, threads, observations) accumulate via declared folds. When the observer decides identity has shifted, they call tick(). The tick payload IS the current identity state. Contributing facts available at full fidelity in the same store. soul.snapshot facts assembled from other loops' fold states feed the soul loop.

Aggregation vertices declare loops as canonical schema. New instances derive base loops from aggregation root, not first sibling. _extract_loops_text brace-matches the block; _find_source_vertex prefers it over instance fallback.
Formalize read processes first (ls), then let write operations (add/rm) emerge from observed patterns.
ls/add/rm are structural operations uniform across levels. Root: vertices. Vertex: contents/population.
ls works at every level: root shows vertices, vertex shows contents. Zoom progression for fidelity.

Unify help rendering: AppRunner intercepts -h at subcommand level, not just top level. Action commands describe args via help_args on AppCommand. Handlers never see -h. Chosen over CliRunner wrapper approach — help is an AppRunner concern, execution is a handler concern.

Replace help secondary: bool with min_zoom: Zoom. Each HelpGroup declares when it appears via min_zoom, rendering follows effective-zoom pattern (eff < 0 hidden, 0 compact, 1 expanded, >= 2 expanded+detail). Dissolves hardcoded Interaction rules section.

SqliteStore (engine) now uses ULID primary keys matching libs/store schema. Single canonical schema: id TEXT PK DEFAULT (ulid()), json_valid CHECK on payload. Existing rowid-based stores migrated manually. No backwards-compat shim — ULID is the only path forward.

Vertex instances ARE the templates — dissolved. No separate template layer. dev/ namespace at config level holds reference vertices, but they're just instances that happen to be reusable. Template dissolution: if the instance is the template, the template concept disappears.

'loops init' is incremental and composable. 'loops init project' creates a project vertex, 'loops init check' adds a check vertex later. Each init is self-contained — not monolithic templates or cookiecutter scaffolding.

discover and combine serve different purposes and don't overlap. discover is structural/navigational — finding what exists. combine is data — reading through to referenced stores via ATTACH + UNION ALL.

'combine' block with absolute paths to vertex files replaces discover+symlink pattern for data aggregation. combine is the data path (reads through to referenced stores), discover stays structural/navigational.

'.loops/' is the project-local home for vertices and data. Vertices are project identity (checked in), data is gitignored by *.db pattern. loops init writes to .loops/, vertex discovery checks .loops/ first with cwd fallback.

Gutter is a continuous vertical rail. GutterFn returns primary weight. Stepping map derives continuation weight: full block to half, half to thin, thin stays thin. Color stays same, weight steps down. Rail never breaks.

PayloadLens returns the summary (str or styled Block). record_line owns continuation lines — DETAILED shows well-known secondary fields, FULL shows all fields. Lenses must not embed multiline content; record_line handles that.

record_line and record_line_composed are production rendering primitives in painted. Gutter continuity (stepping weight), shallow 2-char indent, Block.column() constructor, width-constrained FULL output. health_lens returns summary string, record_line handles continuation lines.

Sources block takes an execution mode. 'sources sequential' runs sources in declaration order with exit-on-failure gating (non-zero exit code stops subsequent sources). Bare sources at vertex level remain concurrent (today's default). The general form is: sources block takes a mode keyword — sequential today, concurrent implicit default. DAG or other patterns become additional modes, not different mechanisms. This dissolves health.py's run_checks() into a vertex declaration — the imperative Python code that runs subprocesses sequentially and gates on failure becomes a property of the source execution model. The lens and view (health_lens, health_view, gutter_pass_fail) survive as rendering concerns. check.vertex replaces the ./dev check shell scripts with a vertex declaration: sources sequential { source 'ruff...' { kind lint.result } source 'pytest...' { kind test.result } } with folds collecting latest per step.

Seven dataclasses identified for future freeze: Source (atoms), CompiledVertex (engine), Tap (engine), Shape (lang), Location (lang), CellWrite (painted), CliRunner (painted). Three legitimately mutable: ValidationContext (error accumulator), Loop (timing state), FrameRecord (if exists in painted timer).

Three new AST-enforced rules in test_architecture.py: (5) frozen dataclasses in all libs — 10 exceptions whitelisted, 7 marked TODO-freeze; (6) atoms stdlib-only — zero external runtime deps; (7) sqlite3 confined to engine and store. Non-AST-enforceable invariants documented: display_width vs len (needs type info), parameterized SQL (already clean), bare except (too rare).

Phase 1: AST CombineEntry + combine parsing + validation + _combined_read in vertex_reader (branch on ast.combine). Phase 2: CLI --combine flag for temporary vertices + loops init --combine for persistent. Phase 3: provenance tagging (deferred).

Combinatorial vertices use a 'combine' block in vertex KDL — mutually exclusive with 'store'. References other vertices by name (not store paths). No store, no discover, no sources. CombineEntry AST node. Temporary vertices constructed in memory via --combine CLI flag.

Combinatorial vertices virtualize via SQLite ATTACH DATABASE at read time — UNION ALL facts across stores, replay through declared folds. No materialization, no copied data, always fresh. Materialization remains available via existing merge_store as explicit user choice.

The full rendering vocabulary for the personal intelligence system is now designed. Four composition primitives: record_line (atomic timestamped record), record_timeline (temporal grouping by date), record_map (topological grouping by key hierarchy), and canvas/DAG/graph (spatial — positioned nodes with edges). Two modifier types compose with all primitives: GutterFn maps (kind, payload) to (char, style) for colored left edge, AttentionFn maps (kind, payload) to float 0-1 for information-gain dimming/highlighting. PayloadLens maps (kind, payload, zoom) to str|Block for domain-specific content rendering. The universal formula: a view is record_{timeline,map,canvas} + PayloadLens + GutterFn? + AttentionFn?. Everything else is a parameter on one of these. Four GutterFn implementations (lifecycle, freshness, pass_fail, observer). Four AttentionFn implementations (staleness, novelty, blocked, relevance). Demo code proves all combinations across three scenarios (task board, decision map, siftd views).

Prototype DAG lens before canvas or graph lens. Four reasons: (1) Tasks already have explicit dependency edges (blocks/blocked_by) — no graph inference needed. (2) DAGs have natural top-to-bottom flow — simplest layout algorithm (topological sort + git-style column assignment). (3) Git-style column rendering needs zero new Painted primitives — join_vertical of row-blocks, same as tree_lens. (4) Immediate use case in strange-loops task visualization. Phase sequence: DAG lens (git-style columns, no new primitives) → canvas() primitive → Canvas lens (grid layout, Manhattan routing, store topology) → Graph lens (Sugiyama layout, fact dependency chains, cross-vertex references). Each phase builds on the prior.

The canvas() composition primitive bridges Painted's static lens path to 2D positioning. Painted already has Buffer-level absolute positioning (Block.paint(buf, x, y)) but only in interactive Surface mode. canvas(width, height, placements) uses Buffer internally to compose Placement(x, y, block) entries, then extracts an immutable Block. No new rendering path — just a new composition function. draw_edge(buf, path, style) adds box-drawing polylines with Manhattan routing. Layout algorithms live in the lens, not in Painted — the library provides the rendering primitive, domain-specific layout stays in domain code. This enables three new lens types: DAG (directed flow), Canvas (2D positioned nodes with edges), and Graph (relationship visualization). All compose with existing modifiers (gutter, attention) and respect zoom levels.

Three composition primitives for rendering vertex data, each matching a different data shape. (1) record_timeline — groups by date, temporal data. Natural for session logs, task activity, change history. Already built in painted-log-line-demo. (2) record_map — groups by key hierarchy (namespace tree), topological data. Natural for decision landscapes (testing/*, architecture/*), tag views, observer groupings. Zoom: MINIMAL = group names + counts, SUMMARY = group + latest record, DETAILED = all records within groups. Being designed now. (3) record_line — the atomic unit. Single timestamped record at a zoom level. All composition primitives delegate to record_line for individual records. PayloadLens and modifiers apply at record_line level and propagate through any composition primitive. A fourth primitive — spatial/graph/canvas — is under research for relationship data (fact dependency chains, store topology, cross-project impact).

Six design principles from log line research ground the rendering vocabulary. (1) Color encodes exactly one dimension — severity OR source, never both. (2) INFO is baseline, color marks deviation — journalctl principle. (3) Fixed-width metadata columns + variable message = scannable — Docker Compose pattern. (4) Severity gutter is underused in terminals — Grafana/Datadog colored left edge, minimal footprint, instantly scannable. (5) Continuation indentation beats prefix repetition for multi-line — lnav principle. (6) Width-aware rendering is a gap in terminal tools — an opportunity for Painted to own. Additionally: the PayloadLens pattern (kind, payload, zoom) → str|Block is the domain extension point. Default rendering handles unknown kinds via well-known key extraction. Domain lenses (loops_lens, siftd_lens) override with kind-specific formatting. Lenses compose with modifiers — the lens provides content, the modifier provides visual treatment.

Lens rendering composes through modifiers, not separate lens implementations. Four modifier types identified from the painted-log-line-demo research: (1) Gutter — colored left edge encoding one dimension (lifecycle stage, health, freshness). Follows Grafana/Datadog pattern, underused in terminals. (2) Attention — dim low-information-gain records, highlight high. First-occurrence errors are high attention, 47th occurrence is noise. Decisions on new topics are high, routine ticks are low. This connects to the attention-filter design. (3) History arc — show evolution over time (thread exploring→active→decided, experiment proposed→graduated). Records are trajectories, not points. (4) Density — vertical space per record, controlled by Zoom enum. These compose freely: task board = record_timeline + gutter(lifecycle) + attention(staleness). Thread pipeline = record_timeline + history_arc + attention(readiness). Channel response = attention(high_only) + density(MINIMAL).

Apps register three things: lenses (read fold state → render at fidelity levels), feedback handlers (user input → emit fact/tick), and action observers (react to intent facts with side effects). The harness makes all three available as both CLI commands and TUI keybindings at appropriate fidelity. There is no separate app scaffold per domain — the directory structure, CLI dispatch, dev harness are identical. What differentiates siftd from strange-loops from hlab is: which vertex declaration (template), which lenses (Painted components), which feedback/action handlers (domain logic). The registration mechanism is the app's interface to the system.

Actions with side effects (task send spawns a worktree, task merge runs git operations) are not special-cased command handlers — they are facts on the loop. Emitting task.dispatch places the intent on the wire; something observes it and executes the side effect; the outcome emits as a further fact; the boundary folds the cascade. The CLI command and TUI keypress are different input surfaces for the same emission. This is not the mature future form — it is the design. If we build it this way from the start, the system composes: any observer can react to any fact, boundaries track lifecycle across the full cascade, and the audit trail is complete. Custom domain logic that does not dissolve — worktree management, subprocess spawning, git operations — lives in the observer/tick handler that reacts to the intent fact, not in the CLI command that emits it.

The store-ingest-design plan dissolves. Ingest — format-aware file reading that produces facts — is not a library primitive. It decomposes into: Sources (shell commands or scripts that read files and emit NDJSON, already exist), parse pipeline (dict→dict shaping vocabulary, already exists), and vertex declaration (kind/fold configuration, already exists). siftd's adapters are Sources that haven't been written as Sources yet — each adapter's discover→parse pipeline is equivalent to a script that reads conversation files and emits structured records. The three-tier loading (built-in < entry point < drop-in) becomes: template ships default source scripts, user overrides with project-local scripts. The read_file/ingest_to_store API proposed for libs/store is unnecessary — format-aware file reading is a script concern, not a library primitive. Bulk historical import is an edge case solved by a script, not a library. What remains from the store-operations-design plan (receive, compact, transport) is genuine new infrastructure — moving stores between locations does not dissolve into existing primitives.

Apps dissolve into lenses + feedback over vertex declarations. An app does not own a data model or ingress pipeline — the vertex declaration (kinds, folds, search fields, boundaries) IS the data model, Sources ARE ingress, and both ship via template at loops init. What an app uniquely provides: (1) domain-specific lenses — Painted rendering of fold state tuned to a data shape (conversation browser, task board, system dashboard), and (2) feedback capability — the ability to emit facts/ticks back through the lens (tag a conversation, close a task, ack an alert). The observer and actor are the same entity. This dissolves siftd, strange-loops, and hlab into structurally identical programs: Painted surfaces over vertex state with domain-specific rendering and keybindings. The Smalltalk parallel is precise — there is one runtime (loops), one data substrate (vertex/store), one rendering framework (Painted), and domain configuration that selects active lenses and feedback handlers. The app is a perspective on the system, not a separate program. Unlike Smalltalk, the Unix grounding holds: stores are cp-able SQLite files, sources are shell commands, CLI is always available as pipe-friendly lower-fidelity surface.

Cross-store search follows recall-then-compose pattern: FTS5 per store independently, union candidate sets, fold/rank across combined results. Composition happens after recall, not during — FTS5 virtual tables are per-database in SQLite. Each vertex store remains self-contained with its own FTS5 index. Combinatorial vertex is a view over stores, not a new store. Search follows the same boundary.

FTS5 indexing uses lazy catch-up pattern: first search pays full backfill, subsequent searches catch up on deltas via high water mark in fts_state table. No INSERT triggers — triggers can't do spec-aware field extraction since the kind-to-field mapping lives in the vertex file, not the database. The resultant first-search slowdown fits the loops philosophy: not necessary that things happen instantly, only that they do reliably with the same data flow and rule set. Idempotent, incremental, spec-driven.

Vertex spec declares searchable fields per kind: search "field1" "field2" as sibling of fold and boundary in loop definitions. LoopDef gains search: tuple[str, ...]. Callers don't override at query time — vertex_search reads the spec to know what's searchable. Only declared fields extracted and indexed. Keeps FTS5 index surgical at scale. Template loop resolution via collect_search_fields() handles generated kind names from .loop templates.
