AST-based architecture boundary tests in tests/test_architecture.py enforce vertex-as-read-interface. Four rules: apps don't import StoreReader (exceptions: store inspector permanent, pop_store temporary), apps don't import sqlite3, libs don't import apps, lib dependency DAG (engine->lang runtime, engine->atoms runtime). TYPE_CHECKING-aware visitor. Stale exceptions fail the test. Monorepo-level concern, lives at repo root not in any single lib.

FTS5 is required infrastructure, not optional. Spec-declared searchable fields in vertex KDL (search "field1" "field2" as sibling of fold/boundary). Standalone FTS5 table with lazy catch-up indexing via high water mark — no INSERT triggers because spec-to-field mapping lives in vertex, not database. Write path untouched; FTS is purely read-side. First search pays backfill cost, subsequent searches catch up on deltas. Combinatorial vertex search: each store searched independently, results merged after recall. json_extract+LIKE rejected — personal scale grows continuously (siftd alone 100-150k+ facts), cross-store combinatorial queries multiply dataset.

painted grows generic record rendering patterns: timestamped record lines, date-grouped timelines, fold-state tables. These are parameterized by a payload lens — painted handles the chrome (timestamps, grouping, zoom degradation, width), the caller provides meaning. Facts, ticks, blog entries, log lines — all are timestamped records with fields. The structural shape is the same; the payload lens provides the domain interpretation.

A payload lens is a single function (kind, payload, zoom) → str | Block. At zoom 0 it returns a one-liner gist. At zoom 2+ it returns a multi-line expansion. content_gist and _log_summary dissolve into this — they were always the same concept at different zoom levels. The structural renderer (fact_line, log_block) composes with the payload lens via a summarize callback. Apps inject domain-specific payload lenses; the default is heuristic field scanning.

Three-layer rendering architecture: (1) painted owns structural primitives (Block, Line, zoom, run_cli), timestamp formatting, and generic data patterns like record timelines — parameterized, domain-agnostic. (2) apps/loops owns store atom rendering (fact_line, tick_line, log_block) that maps facts/ticks to painted's structural patterns, plus the default payload lens (content_gist heuristic). (3) Domain apps provide per-kind payload lenses that override the default. painted never imports from atoms/engine.

Domain-specific lenses generalize to painted when the same rendering pattern appears in 2+ apps. This happens naturally — don't extract ahead of time. Generic store lenses (fact timeline, fold state views) live in painted. Domain lenses (siftd search results, task lifecycle) stay in the app. The signal to extract is independent convergence, not prediction.

An app is: main(argv) entry point, commands/ (fetch functions returning typed dicts), lenses/ (painted views at zoom levels), and wiring through run_cli. fetch → lens → run_cli gives JSON/plain/pipe/live output for free. Apps use vertex_read/vertex_facts for reads, never StoreReader directly (search is a documented exception until libs/store Phase 2).

Apps register a short name in an explicit dict in loops/main.py. loops <app> <args> delegates to the app's main(argv) via importlib. ~5 lines. Phase 1 is a static registry; entry-point discovery deferred until 3+ apps stabilize. The app owns its own command dispatch, fetch functions, and lenses. The boundary is main(argv) — loops CLI doesn't know about app internals, app doesn't know about loops CLI dispatch.

The vertex is the sole read interface for store data. StoreReader becomes an internal detail of how the vertex materializes fold state from its backing store — not a public API. The read path compiles the vertex declaration, replays facts through declared folds, and returns fold state. No vertex declaration for a kind means no way to read that kind. This structurally prevents bypassing fold declarations (same pattern as frozen dataclasses preventing mutation). Enforcement is a tier 1 package boundary check (StoreReader not in public API), not a behavioral test. _latest_by_group in session.py dissolves — the vertex fold compilation already implements FoldBy. loops status and loops log compile the vertex, ask for fold states, render them.
Three experiments confirmed model maps to Bend. Parked — Python more productive. Revisit when Bend2 lands (32/64-bit numbers, native IO).

Progressive CLAUDE.md session: rewrote root CLAUDE.md (monorepo orientation only), created libs/atoms/CLAUDE.md (4 levels: Observe, Accumulate, Shape, Ingest with semantic-first examples), established project.vertex as durable project store, merged vertex-template-system (slashed names, init with config registration, aggregation vertices). Emitted 5 decisions to meta store (progressive-vertex-chain, project-vertex-pattern, distributed-store-default, vertex-templates, domain-vs-knowledge-vertices). Next: engine CLAUDE.md, then lang, painted alignment, store.

Progressive CLAUDE.md for libs/atoms. Four levels: Observe (Fact), Accumulate (Spec+Fold+Boundary), Shape input (Parse vocabulary), Ingest (Source+Runner). Each level has trigger conditions and dont-reach-for-yet barriers. Replaces the 20-line README stub. Deep dive docs (VERTEX.md, TEMPORAL.md) referenced from engine CLAUDE.md, not atoms — they're engine-level concepts.

SUPERSEDED: The merge gap dissolves when strange-loops replaces subtask. Workers emit facts instead of writing artifacts to branches. No branch to orphan, no merge step to miss. See threads: strange-loops-merge-strategy, artifact-to-fact-convergence.

Progressive CLAUDE.md system for the loops monorepo. Applying the painted library pattern (levels with escalation triggers, don't-reach-for-yet barriers) to replace the God CLAUDE.md antipattern. Walking through each level top-down with the user, aligning on content at each step, emitting decisions to project and meta stores as we go. Simultaneously establishing the project vertex as the loops-specific knowledge store alongside the cross-cutting meta store.

Do not add kinds_consumed to Tick atom. Use tool-level scope derived from .vertex config when available, or observer/origin filtering. Avoids per-tick duplication, API ripple, and semantic ambiguity. Design on stale branch design/tick-provenance.

Two kinds of vertex files. Domain vertices are code — checked into repos, define data pipelines (hlab status.vertex, economy.vertex). Knowledge vertices are runtime config — project decisions, threads, tasks, handoff. Domain vertices ship with the app. Knowledge vertices are instantiated from templates via loops init. Both are .vertex files, same format, different lifecycle.

Vertex templates are replicable patterns (project, task, handoff, pickup). loops init <template> creates a local instance in cwd. The config-level template vertex discovers all local instances. Templates define the fold shape; each instantiation gets its own data. Like git repos — local is the primitive, aggregation is the network.

Stores are distributed-first, aggregate-up. Each project gets its own local vertex + SQLite store, self-contained and portable. Config level (~/.config/loops/) is the aggregation layer — template vertices discover all local instances. loops status <name> reads local; loops status dev/<name> reads aggregate. Separate DBs by default. Merge/combinatorial vertex is opt-in aggregation. Same shape as the loops model: local loops produce ticks, ticks flow up. Local stores produce facts, aggregate vertices see them all.

Renamed session.vertex to project.vertex. Project vertex holds durable project-specific decisions, threads, tasks. Same shape as meta vertex but scoped to this repo. Symlinked at ~/.config/loops/project for global access. Meta stays cross-cutting; project stays repo-specific.

Root CLAUDE.md is monorepo orientation only: pointer to LOOPS.md, build commands, structure map, discovery path to lib/app CLAUDE.md files, and project store query instructions. Model details, data flow, lib-specific patterns all pushed down to lib-level CLAUDE.md. Validates claude-md-levels and claude-md-antipatterns decisions from meta.

project.vertex is the durable project-level store for decisions, threads, and tasks. Named 'project' (not 'session') — same pattern strange-loops uses for task orchestration, now a meta-pattern for all projects. Cross-cutting concerns stay in meta; project-specific concerns accumulate in the project vertex. Progressive CLAUDE.md references the project store at each level.

CLAUDE.md progressive chain maps 1:1 to vertex store progressive query. The document is the lens, the store is the state. Root CLAUDE.md references the project vertex; lib CLAUDE.md tells you how to query your slice via topic namespace (atoms/*, engine/*, painted/*). One store, kind-namespaced topics, natural slicing. Same pattern as hlab status — one vertex, multiple sources, scoped by name.

painted is the native display layer for the store, not a rendering utility. Every store operation gets a painted view at all four zoom levels. Apps add domain-specific lenses that interpret facts in their context, but the underlying operations are universal. Domain-specific views generalize back into painted over time as patterns recur (task dashboard → generic dashboard lens, siftd data exploration → generic fold exploration). This is why painted is returning to the monorepo — if loops needs a rendering capability, painted grows to support it.

Transport is a pluggable interface, not a fixed implementation. The operations are constant (slice on source, merge on target), the pipe varies (local, SSH, HTTP, future protocols). Local transport ships first (testable, no infrastructure). SSH next (alcove sync is forcing function). HTTP when scrape/collect patterns emerge (Prometheus-style data collection). Store operations (slice, merge) are transport-agnostic — they work on local paths, transport handles the pipe.

FTS5 full-text search as the starting point for store.search. Zero external dependencies, sub-millisecond query at current scale (100-10k facts). Recursive JSON text extraction reliable by construction (payload-is-JSON contract). json_extract() for field filtering works without indexes up to ~100k facts. Indexed projections (CREATE INDEX on json_extract) are the progressive enhancement when scale demands it — boundary is roughly 100k+ facts or hot query paths.

Payload is always valid JSON — enforced on write. This guarantees json_extract() is always safe, FTS can always walk the tree, and indexed columns can be projected from payload fields. Progressive enhancement: add explicit indexed columns (CREATE INDEX on json_extract) for hot query paths as needed.

Facts and ticks use ULID as primary key via native SQLite extension (not application-generated like siftd did). Globally unique, time-ordered, sortable. Merge becomes INSERT OR IGNORE — ULID is the identity across stores. Replaces auto-increment rowid. Cursor semantics preserved (ULID is sortable, WHERE id > last_seen still works). Essential for distributed store orchestration.

meta-discussion is the design space around loops itself, not just decisions about projects. Cross-cutting architecture (store operations, combinatorial vertices, attention filters, context folds, pickup/winddown workflow) emerges here and in cross-instance channels (alcove discord), not in the loops repo. Parallel meta-discussion on the meta aspect of loops along with ways of working.

Single-project pickup is multi-fold reads on one vertex — not a combinatorial vertex. Read fold(handoff, latest) + fold(threads, active) + fold(decisions, recent) from one store. Cross-project pickup is where combinatorial vertices enter — reading folds across N stores. libs/store dissolves the boundary between these two scales.

Pickup and wind-down converge with alcove's context fold pattern. Pickup is a lens on the meta vertex (handoff prose + threads + tasks + decisions). Wind-down emits the handoff fact + reconciles the store. Alcove's context fold is the same shape — read all stores, compose a view for orientation. The general pattern: any session boundary (start, end, dispatch) needs a lens over accumulated state.

Cross-project queryability dissolves into store operations once libs/store exists. Emit a decision in meta, discover it from loops — not via special cross-project protocol, but via store merge/search/transport. The combinatorial vertex is the query mechanism.

Three-document architecture proven on alcove: SOUL.md (identity — editorial, hand-shaped), CLAUDE.md (operational — tools, paths, permissions), loops store (learned knowledge — accumulated facts, foldable, queryable). MEMORY.md is a cache/projection of operational knowledge for session start, not the source of truth.

Alcove's context fold pattern: before a message hits the agent, the dispatch loop constructs a brief context summary — latest activity across all channels/stores. Lightweight (just latest timestamps + one line per channel). Same pattern as continuous-session-emission for claude-code sessions. The fold is ephemeral, rebuilt every dispatch.

New loops lib explicitly for store operations. Absorbs from siftd: ingest (format-specific readers, schema-agnostic output), slice (by date/project/tag), merge (combine stores, deduplicate), search (FTS5 + semantic over any SQLite store), transport (push/pull/sync between stores over SSH or HTTP). Generic over any vertex store.

Dynamically compose vertices that fold across multiple underlying stores — e.g. alcove.db + kyle.db + watchers.db into a temporary vertex for a specific query. Temporary by default, persisted if the pattern proves useful. This is what makes cross-store search fundamental rather than a feature.

siftd decomposes into loops monorepo. Sources are already solved (harnesses are just source definitions). New libs/store absorbs all DB operations siftd proved out: ingest, slice, merge, search, transport. siftd-as-app becomes a thin specialization layer — LLM harness configs + analytics queries + CLI. Same relationship as painted (rendering spine) — siftd becomes the analytics spine.

Wind-down has two phases: reconciliation (emit missing facts, resolve completed threads, update tasks) and handoff (prose synthesis). Scaffold elements are Ticks derived from decision namespaces. Boundary must be manual (deliberate snapshot at project init), not automatic cascading. Gets observability that templates don't — can trace propagation and divergence.

Keep HANDOFF.md as prose for cold-start reorientation. Derive decisions from it as loops facts. Prose is the source document, facts are the queryable index. Two representations serving different access patterns.
loops is operating memory (what do we believe right now?), siftd is the archaeological layer (what actually happened, what can we learn from it?). Complementary, not converging.
Session facts are source of truth for state. Documents (LOG.md) are source of truth for understanding. Could generate HANDOFF.md from facts, but not a good LOG.md — that requires narrative judgment.
HANDOFF.md is the critical document for returning from a gap. Must answer: what was I doing, what's next, what's deferred, what's the current state. It's the bridge across the wave's trough.

Each doc type has a different audience and update cadence. CLAUDE.md: working context, changes when conventions change. README.md: external users. HANDOFF.md: session continuity, rewritten each session. LOG.md: history, grows every session. DESIGN.md: architecture, written once updated rarely. VOCABULARY.md: definitions.

God CLAUDE.md (everything in root, important things buried — split when section serves only one lib/app). Stale CLAUDE.md (conventions no longer true — arch tests help). Redundant CLAUDE.md (repeating parent — reference up instead). Missing CLAUDE.md (scaffold should include stub).

Four levels: global (~/.config/claude/CLAUDE.md) for how you work, root (project/CLAUDE.md) for what the system is + build + structure, lib for one concern + key types + invariants, app for domain mapping + dev cycle + CLI shape. Each level adds, not repeats.

Live-fire trace 2026-04-27 confirmed _fire_live_boundaries (vertex.py:440-504) and evaluate_boundaries (vertex.py:696-802) share IDENTICAL gating logic — same kind check, same match check, same conditions check; only differ in fact source (live: just-arrived fact; catchup: iteration over period_facts from store). Duplication is mechanical not conceptual. evaluate_boundaries' actual job is replay-suppression catchup — facts loaded via replay() with _replaying=True bypass live-fire; evaluate_boundaries re-runs the gates on them once after replay. Belongs in load_vertex_program after vertex.replay(), not in program.sync. Live-fire is complete for every boundary type that exists today: every boundary requires a triggering kind, live-fire fires whenever that kind arrives and the gates (match, conditions) pass. Bonus finding: state-crossing boundaries (fire on fold predicate true regardless of triggering kind) don't exist today and would be the only boundary type requiring evaluation-on-every-state-change. Surfaced as a fourth potential category, then dissolved with the other three into the watcher-triple primitive.
